questions.text: Primitive subsistence farming is also known as 'slash and burn' agriculture. What is it called in Mexico and Central America?
questions.explanation: Slash and burn agriculture is known as Milpa in Mexico and Central America.

questions.text: What is the name of 'slash and burn' agriculture in the North-Eastern states of India like Assam and Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: Jhumming is the common name for primitive subsistence farming in the North-Eastern states.

questions.text: Which type of farming is practiced in areas of high population pressure on land?
questions.explanation: Intensive subsistence farming is labor-intensive and uses high doses of biochemical inputs to get higher production.

questions.text: In which type of farming is a single crop grown on a large area?
questions.explanation: Plantation is a type of commercial farming where a single crop is grown over a vast area using migrant labor.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a plantation crop in India?
questions.explanation: Tea, coffee, rubber, sugarcane, and banana are important plantation crops in India.

questions.text: Rabi crops are sown in:
questions.explanation: Rabi crops are sown in winter and harvested in summer (April to June).

questions.text: Which of the following is a Rabi crop?
questions.explanation: Wheat, barley, peas, gram, and mustard are some of the important Rabi crops.

questions.text: Kharif crops are grown with the onset of:
questions.explanation: Kharif crops are sown with the onset of monsoon in different parts of the country and harvested in September-October.

questions.text: The short season between the Rabi and the Kharif seasons is known as:
questions.explanation: The Zaid season occurs during the summer months; crops include watermelon, muskmelon, and cucumber.

questions.text: India is the second largest producer of ________ in the world after China.
questions.explanation: India is a major producer of rice, which is the staple food crop of a majority of the people in India.

questions.text: Which crop requires high temperature (above 25°C) and high humidity with annual rainfall above 100 cm?
questions.explanation: Rice is a Kharif crop that needs high heat and plenty of water.

questions.text: Which of the following states is a major producer of wheat in India?
questions.explanation: The main wheat-producing states are Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Bihar, and Rajasthan.

questions.text: Which of the following crops are collectively known as millets?
questions.explanation: Jowar, Bajra, and Ragi are the important millets grown in India, often called coarse grains but having high nutritional value.

questions.text: Which millet is very rich in iron, calcium, other micro-nutrients and roughage?
questions.explanation: Ragi is a crop of dry regions and is highly nutritious.

questions.text: Which crop is used both as food and fodder?
questions.explanation: Maize is a Kharif crop (though in Bihar it is grown in Rabi) used for both human consumption and animal feed.

questions.text: India is the largest producer as well as the consumer of ________ in the world.
questions.explanation: Pulses are the major source of protein in a vegetarian diet.

questions.text: Which of the following is a leguminous crop?
questions.explanation: All pulses (except arhar) help in restoring soil fertility by fixing nitrogen from the air.

questions.text: Which crop takes almost a year to grow?
questions.explanation: Sugarcane is a tropical as well as a subtropical crop that takes a long time to mature.

questions.text: India is the largest producer of ________ in the world (as of 2020 statistics).
questions.explanation: India produces various oilseeds covering about 12% of the total cropped area.

questions.text: Which oilseed is a Kharif crop in north India and Rabi crop in south India?
questions.explanation: Sesamum (Til) has different growing seasons depending on the geography of India.

questions.text: Tea cultivation is an example of ________ agriculture.
questions.explanation: Tea is an important beverage crop introduced in India initially by the British.

questions.text: Which variety of coffee was initially brought from Yemen and is produced in India?
questions.explanation: The Arabica variety of coffee is in great demand all over the world.

questions.text: Where was coffee cultivation initially introduced in India?
questions.explanation: Initially, coffee was confined to the Baba Budan Hills in Karnataka.

questions.text: Horticulture refers to the cultivation of:
questions.explanation: India is a producer of tropical as well as temperate fruits and vegetables.

questions.text: Which crop is known as the 'Golden Fibre'?
questions.explanation: Jute is called the golden fibre because of its shiny brown color and high value.

questions.text: Rearing of silk worms for the production of silk fibre is known as:
questions.explanation: Sericulture involves the management of silkworms for silk production.

questions.text: Cotton grows well in which type of soil?
questions.explanation: Black soil of the Deccan plateau is ideal for cotton cultivation.

questions.text: How many frost-free days does cotton require for its growth?
questions.explanation: Cotton requires high temperature, light rainfall, and 210 frost-free days.

questions.text: Who started the Bhoodan-Gramdan movement, also known as the 'Blood-less Revolution'?
questions.explanation: Vinoba Bhave initiated this movement to encourage voluntary land reforms.

questions.text: The Green Revolution was based on the use of:
questions.explanation: High Yielding Variety (HYV) seeds and technology significantly increased crop production.

questions.text: What does KCC stand for in the context of Indian agriculture?
questions.explanation: KCC is a credit scheme introduced by the Government of India for the benefit of farmers.

questions.text: The scheme providing insurance to farmers against crop failure is:
questions.explanation: Personal Accident Insurance Scheme (PAIS) is one of the schemes introduced for farmers.

questions.text: Which of the following is a non-food crop?
questions.explanation: Rubber is an important industrial raw material and is a non-food crop.

questions.text: Which crop is mainly used in the manufacture of gunny bags and ropes?
questions.explanation: Jute is used in making gunny bags, mats, ropes, yarn, and other artifacts.

questions.text: The declaration of Minimum Support Price (MSP) is done to:
options: Provide incentives to farmers and check middle-men
questions.explanation: MSP ensures that farmers get a guaranteed price for their produce, protecting them from market fluctuations.

questions.text: Organic farming is in vogue because:
options: It is practiced without factory-made chemicals like fertilizers
questions.explanation: Organic farming uses natural inputs and is considered environmentally friendly.

questions.text: What is the rank of India in the production of fruits and vegetables in the world?
questions.explanation: India is the second largest producer of fruits and vegetables in the world.

questions.text: Which state is the leading producer of Jowar in India?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra is the largest producer of Jowar, followed by Karnataka.

questions.text: The 'White Revolution' is associated with the production of:
questions.explanation: White Revolution (Operation Flood) aimed to increase milk production in India.

questions.text: What is the major challenge faced by Indian farmers due to globalization?
options: Inability to compete with subsidized agriculture of developed nations
questions.explanation: Indian agricultural products often face tough competition from developed countries where agriculture is highly subsidized.
